items.title: DV shelter death inquest hears from top police, child safety representatives

children.content: A coronial inquest hears that Northern Territory authorities assessed that a father posed "no risk" to his child, despite allegations he repeatedly abused his partner and shook the baby.

image.altText: Darwin local court interior.

items.title: Boy, 16, killed, two others injured in Wadeye quad bike crash

children.content: A 16-year-old boy has been killed in a quad bike crash that also injured a 12-year-old boy and a 19-year-old man in Wadeye on Wednesday.

image.altText: Close up image of an NT Police sign, behind barbed wire fence, with left of frame slightly obstructed by palm tree leaves.

items.title: Kingpin's private phone calls break open Mongols' 'culture of silence'

children.content: Detectives say bikies are recruiting inside Northern Territory prisons, as a kingpin's tapped private phone calls offer a rare glimpse into the underground world of outlaw motorcycle gangs.

gallery.altText: A red white and blue skull embroidered into patches sewn into vests.

thumbnail.altText: A red white and blue skull embroidered into patches sewn into vests.

items.title: 'How many beers have you had?' Flood evacuees claim racial profiling

children.content: Flood evacuees from remote Northern Territory communities say they are being "treated like prisoners" by security guards working at their emergency accommodation centre.

image.altText: Signs attached to black fencing reads: no alcohol, no visitors

items.title: Police 'not fully prepared' for violent scene they were called to, NT inquest hears

children.content: An inquest hears evidence from a senior police officer who ran into a room to disarm a mother who was self-harming inside a Darwin crisis accommodation facility, describing the incident as "one of the worst days" of her policing career.

image.altText: A police car outside the Darwin Local Court.

items.title: 101yo Bombing of Darwin veteran wants Australians to know what unfolded

children.content: At 101 years old, Bombing of Darwin survivor Terrance Byrne wants Australians to know exactly what unfolded during the World War II attack on Darwin — still the single largest foreign attack on Australian soil.

gallery.altText: An older man sitting in front of table of framed black and white pictures.
A black and white photo shows a huge crater in the ground, alongside three undamaged oil tanks located near the coastline.
An army crew operating a large searchlight, the uniformed men are looking towards and pointing up at the night sky.

thumbnail.altText: An older man sitting in front of table of framed black and white pictures.
A black and white photo shows a huge crater in the ground, alongside three undamaged oil tanks located near the coastline.
An army crew operating a large searchlight, the uniformed men are looking towards and pointing up at the night sky.

items.title: New $30.5m funding package to help repair NT roads damaged by flooding

children.content: The Northern Territory government has announced $30.5 million in funding to repair roads damaged by wet season flooding across the territory.

image.altText: A remote road collapsed to one side.

items.title: Housing plan aims to attract more students to international 'gateway' city

children.content: A towering 14-storey addition to Darwin's skyline is expected to ease housing stress for university students, with $70 million invested to tackle the city's competitive rental market.

image.altText: A sketch of a multi-storey building based in the city, the CDU logo can be seen on the side of the accommodation tower.

items.title: Central Australian community sues government over climate-unsafe housing

children.content: Residents of the Central Australian community of Papunya say scorching outback summers are killing their people in unsafe housing. Now they're suing the Northern Territory government to try to force change.

gallery.altText: A drone shot of a small remote community, which features a small number of homes built on the red dirt landscape.
An Indigenous woman and two children sitting on a mattress that rests on the flood inside a home.
A box air conditioner on the outside of a house, balanced on an ironing board and a table.

thumbnail.altText: A drone shot of a small remote community, which features a small number of homes built on the red dirt landscape.
An Indigenous woman and two children sitting on a mattress that rests on the flood inside a home.
A box air conditioner on the outside of a house, balanced on an ironing board and a table.

items.title: Award-winning author who put Brisbane on the literary map dies aged 92

children.content: Erudite and urbane, David Malouf — perhaps most famous for his portrait of mid-century Brisbane in his 1975 novel Johnno — will be fondly remembered for his immeasurable contribution to Australian letters.

image.altText: Author David Malouf.

items.title: Three children attacked by dingoes in national park

children.content: Three children have been attacked by dingoes in Karijini National Park since Saturday, prompting a warning from authorities.

image.altText: A dingo howling towards the camera

items.title: Canavan's frosty foray into Farrer's campaign trail

children.content: This week the Albanese government pushed out a major reform ahead of the federal budget, the Nationals leader was out in the cold on the campaign trail and a former Treasury secretary had some choice words about the push for gas tax reform.

image.altText: Matt Canavan farrer

items.title: Research for Congress raises doubts over AUKUS submarines

children.content: "Worrisome" construction delays plague the US Navy's submarine-building program, which Australia is relying on for its naval fleet.

image.altText: Several crew members walk on top of a submerged, docked submarine.

images.altText: A photo of a helicopter landing on an oil tanker.
Army personnel line into a helicopter.

articles.title: The battle of the blockades in the Strait of Hormuz

children.content: As part of its blockade of Iran, ships have been seized by US forces, others fired upon by Iran and, most recently, boarded by the regime's revolutionary guards. This is all while both sides maintain there is a ceasefire.

image.altText: Masked men in sand-coloured army fatigues climbing a ladder off the side of a ship.

articles.title: 'If only': Indonesian minister floats toll on key international shipping route

children.content: The suggestion of levying ships using the Strait of Malacca was suggested and then walked back by Indonesia's finance minister, but has prompted a response from Canberra.

image.altText: Dozens of cargo vessels stationed in a water way

children.content: Donald Trump says he has ordered the US Navy to "shoot and kill any boat" laying mines in the Strait of Hormuz. Follow live.

items.title: Sheraz risked his life translating in Afghanistan. His family paid the price

children.content: Sheraz worked with Australian and US troops in Afghanistan, as a result his family were targeted by the Taliban. This Anzac Day he's being heroed.

image.altText: A man seated at a table, smiling.

items.title: Russell Brand admits to sex with 16yo when he was 30

children.content: British actor and comedian Russell Brand, who is facing multiple sexual assault charges, has admitted to having sex with a 16-year-old when he was 30.

image.altText: A man dressing black with a pair of sunglasses on

items.title: Curnow kicks a bag in big Swans win as Bulldogs star suffers horror fall

children.content: Sydney's hold on top spot is strengthened by a 66-point win over the Western Bulldogs, whose injury woes continue.

image.altText: Charlie Curnow pumps his fist in celebration near Bulldogs players

items.title: Genesis Owusu goes out of his way to connect with fans — and detractors

children.content: Genesis Owusu today releases his new track Life Keeps Going from his upcoming third album Redstar Wu & the Worldwide Scourge, as he encourages engagement with people who don't agree with his political views.

image.altText: Genesis Owusu bare-chested on stage singing into a microphone, yellow background, Kente cloth from waist down

items.title: The 'plunk' sound of rain makes seeds sprout faster, scientists find

children.content: Scientists say they have the first direct evidence that seeds can sense the sound of rain, and will sprout faster when they do.

image.altText: Green seedling shoots covered in tiny water droplets in dark soil.

items.title: Small WA town takes centre stage for Netflix series filming

children.content: There is a buzz about town in a small community north of Perth as filming begins for a new Netflix series.

image.altText: A film crew in front of beige buildings.

items.title: War on hoons

children.content: You might have heard of hooning – in which people — usually young men — drift, race, and do burnouts on public roads.
It happens across the country and has been around for decades. But, it’s getting bigger, louder and more visible.
So are police crackdowns. Fines, car seizures and jail terms are all on the cards for people caught hooning.
Despite the laws and serious dangers, people still do it. But, why?

image.altText: Blue backgroun with white text. Text reads Inside Australia's illegal drift racing world. ABC News Loop logo

gallery.altText: Blue backgroun with white text. Text reads Inside Australia's illegal drift racing world. ABC News Loop logo

thumbnail.altText: Blue backgroun with white text. Text reads Inside Australia's illegal drift racing world. ABC News Loop logo

items.title: Kyle Sandilands repeatedly bullied colleagues, countersuit alleges

children.content: The company that broadcast the Kyle and Jackie O Show has launched a counter claim against the duo seeking damages for breach of contract, losses in profit and advertising revenue, and legal costs.

image.altText: A selfie of Jackie O Henderson smiling with Kyle Sandilands in the background wearing radio headphones.

items.title: Billionaire's ex-staffer denied bail over $1.6m fraud allegations

children.content: Billionaire Judith Neilson's former secretary is accused of going on a $1.6m shopping spree using her employer's credit cards and bank information.

image.altText: Ms Neilson with white hair cut in a bob looking at the camera with a neutral expression beside a window.

items.title: Authors leave publisher after kids book scrapped for illustrator's comments

children.content: Writers including Evelyn Araluen and Randa Abdel-Fattah have left University of Queensland Press after the publisher axed publication of an Indigenous kids book over its illustrator's past "anti-Semitic" comments.

image.altText: A young woman with cropped hair, wearing large gold earrings and a scarf over her shoulders.

children.content: The incident happened north of Copenhagen, with images showing two yellow and grey trains, both with visible damage.

image.altText: Two yellow trains crashed.

items.title: Homeless people told to surrender dogs in return for housing

children.content: Steve James said he was given the choice between surrendering his dog or continuing to be homeless.

items.title: Fight over legal costs from Gina Rinehart judgement begins

children.content: A legal wrangle is underway over what costs mining heiress Gina Rinehart is liable for following last week's judgement in her epic court battle with the family of her father's former business partner.

image.altText: Gina Rinehart smiles while standing in front of cattle pens

items.title: Author of damning report into CFMEU says he did not keep 'trivial' records

children.content: The inquiry has been investigating alleged misconduct and violence within the Queensland branch of the Construction, Forestry and Maritime Employees Union (CFMEU).

image.altText: A bald man wearing glasses and a suit sitting in front of microphones

items.title: 'We're not ready to leave': Inside the messy dynamic of a no-grounds eviction

children.content: Evicting a tenant without cause offers landlords a simple way out, but the issue can be much more complex than it appears. One such example of that complexity is Eleanor Kahlo's case of her no-grounds eviction.

image.altText: A man with a vest on and a clipboard watches as Eleanor walks through a hallway.

items.title: Every Glock handgun issued to Qld police to be tested

children.content: The Glock pistol has been identified to have a potential fault that could cause them to fire multiple shots instead of one.

image.altText: Close up of Queensland Police Service-issued black gun in holster on navy pants

items.title: New grapevine virus GINV detected for first time in Australia

children.content: A grapevine virus known in China and Japan that affects plant growth and productivity has been detected in Australia for the first time in Victorian, Queensland and South Australian table grapes.

items.title: E-7 Wedgetails are world leading, so why are they up for replacement?

children.content: Australia's fleet of world-leading surveillance aircraft is set to be replaced and analysts say the planes could be replaced by drones.

image.altText: A plane flies over a city

items.title: Farmers say an easy solution to the mouse plague has been rejected

children.content: Farmers are pushing for stronger bait approval as mice numbers soar out of control in Western Australia, but stronger poisons
are being rejected by the national chemical regulator.

image.altText: Feral mice in a bucket.

items.title: This man plays the last post to a different fallen Anzac every day
